title: Genomic DNA Removal and Total RNA Purification from Lysate
author: Bioline
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f48bqzw
materials: ISOLATE II Genomic DNA Removal Column

steps.title: Assemble an ISOLATE II Genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: Assemble an ISOLATE II Genomic DNA Removal Column (blue ring) with a Collection Tube (provided). Ensure you use the correct column: the Genomic DNA Removal Column has a blue ring.

steps.title: Apply up to 600 μL of...
steps.instruction: Apply up to 600 μL of lysate prepared from section 8 on to the column and centrifuge for 1 min at 14,000 x g. 01:00:00 Note: Ensure that the entire lysate volume has passed into the Collection Tube by inspecting the column. If the entire lysate volume has not passed through, centrifuge for an additional minute at 14,000 x g.

steps.title: Transfer the flow-through into a sterile RNase-free 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the flow-through into a sterile RNase-free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (user supplied) for Total RNA Purification.

steps.title: The flow-through contains the RNA and...
steps.instruction: The flow-through contains the RNA and should be stored on ice or at -20°C until the Total RNA Purification protocol is carried out.

steps.title: Discard the column with the bound genomic DNA
steps.instruction: Discard the column with the bound genomic DNA.

steps.title: To every 100 μL of flow-through,...
steps.instruction: To every 100 μL of flow-through, add 60 μL of 96-100% ethanol. Ensure you use the correct column: the ISOLATE II RNA Column has a black ring. For example, for 200 μL of flow-through, add 120 μL of ethanol.

steps.title: Mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Assemble an ISOLATE II RNA Column...
steps.instruction: Assemble an ISOLATE II RNA Column (black ring) with a provided Collection Tube.

steps.title: Apply up to 600 μL of...
steps.instruction: Apply up to 600 μL of the ethanolic lysate onto the column and centrifuge for 1 min at ≥3,500 x g. 00:01:00 Note: Ensure the entire lysate volume has passed into the Collection Tube by inspecting the column. If the entire lysate volume has not passed through, centrifuge for an additional minute at 14,000 x g.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Reassemble the spin column with its Collection Tube
steps.instruction: Reassemble the spin column with its Collection Tube.

steps.title: Depending on the lysate volume, repeat steps 9-11 as required
steps.instruction: Depending on the lysate volume, repeat steps 9-11 as required.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: The ISOLATE II Biofluids RNA Kit isolates Total RNA with minimal amounts of genomic DNA contamination. However, for sensitive applications, an optional on-column DNA removal protocol is provided in Appendix A. DNase I treatment should be performed at this point in the protocol with the supplied DNase I. For highly sensitive applications, in-solution DNase I treatment can be performed (see Optional On-Column DNase I Treatment ).

steps.title: Apply 400 μL of Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Apply 400 μL of Wash Buffer W1 to the RNA Column and centrifuge for 1 min at 14,000 x g. (wash 1/3) 00:01:00 Note: Ensure the entire wash buffer volume has passed into the Collection Tube by inspecting the column. If the entire wash buffer volume has not passed through, centrifuge for an additional minute at 14,000 x g.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and reassemble the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with the Collection Tube. (wash 1/3)

steps.title: Apply 400 μL of Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Apply 400 μL of Wash Buffer W1 to the RNA Column and centrifuge for 1 min at 14,000 x g. (wash 2/3) 00:01:00

steps.title: Wash column a third time by...
steps.instruction: Wash column a third time by adding another 400 μL of Wash Buffer W1 and centrifuge for 1 min at 14,000 x g. 00:01:00

steps.title: Discard flow-through and reassemble spin column with its Collection Tube
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through and reassemble spin column with its Collection Tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 min at 14,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 min at 14,000 x g in order to dry the column thoroughly. Discard the Collection Tube. 00:02:00

steps.title: Place the RNA Column into a fresh 1
steps.instruction: Place the RNA Column into a fresh 1.7 mL Elution Tube (supplied).

steps.title: Add 50 μL of RNA Elution Buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of RNA Elution Buffer to the column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 min at 200...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 min at 200 x g, followed by 1 min at 14,000 x g. Note the volume eluted from the column. 00:02:00

steps.title: If the entire volume has not...
steps.instruction: If the entire volume has not been eluted, spin column for an additional minute at 14,000 x g to elute the RNA. 00:01:00 Note: For maximum RNA recovery, it is recommended to apply a second volume of RNA Elution Buffer and elute into the same microcentrifuge tube (repeat steps 22 - 24). Alternatively, re-apply the first eluate onto the column and re-elute into the same microcentrifuge tube (for high concentration).

steps.title: The isolated RNA can be stored...
steps.instruction: The isolated RNA can be stored at -20°C for a few days or at -80°C (recommended) for long- term storage.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and reassemble the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and reassemble the spin column with the Collection Tube. (wash 2/3)
